what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG trash can icon using currentColor stroked outline paths with no fill.
observations: Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor.
Uses stroke-width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
Draws the trash can lid, handle, body outline, and two inner vertical lines with two path elements.
standouts: The icon is built entirely from stroked path geometry.
The asset has no embedded title, description, class, or id attributes.
conventions: Follows common outline-icon conventions: 24x24 viewBox, no fill, round caps, round joins, and currentColor stroke.
Keeps the SVG self-contained and free of external references.
Uses inline path data rather than separate shape elements for each visible part.
concerns: No title or desc element is present in the SVG itself.
No explicit accessibility attributes are present in the SVG itself.
notes: A compact reusable trash/delete outline icon asset that inherits its rendered color from context.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming markup instead of inside this SVG?
Is the 1.5 stroke width part of a broader icon stroke convention?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a trash icon, and the SVG content draws a trash can outline.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable trash/delete action icon that fits a 24x24 icon grid and adapts to surrounding text or UI color.
contribution: Provides a scalable, color-inheriting trash/delete icon asset with simple stroked geometry for remove or discard actions.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 SVG trash can outline icon using currentColor stroke, no fill, round stroke styling, and two path elements.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a delete, trash, discard, or remove action as a reusable vector icon.
